message:
fix(indexer,bd-wxsy8): close StreamingByteLimiter lost-wakeup race

Pre-fix, src/indexer/mod.rs::StreamingByteLimiter::update_max_bytes_in_flight
stored the new max via AtomicUsize::store + cv.notify_all WITHOUT
acquiring the self.state lock that acquire_with_wait holds during
predicate evaluation. This is the standard lost-wakeup interleaving:

  T1 (waiter): holds state-lock, reads max=5, evaluates predicate
               (bytes_in_flight=10, request=3, no fit), drops lock,
               ↘  about to call cv.wait
  T2 (updater): stores max=20 atomically + notify_all
                 (waiter not yet parked → notification dropped)
  T1 (waiter): enters cv.wait → parks indefinitely

T1 hangs until the next release() (which may never happen if no other
task is holding bytes — e.g., a single-waiter capacity-shrink-then-grow
under low concurrency).

Fix: acquire self.state lock inside update_max_bytes_in_flight before
storing and notifying. This is the standard correct condvar protocol —
the predicate update must happen INSIDE the lock the waiter holds
during predicate evaluation. Critical-section cost is one atomic store;
notify_all under the lock is safe (the waiter is woken after the lock
drops).

Regression test:
- streaming_byte_limiter_update_does_not_lose_wakeup_under_repeated_shrink_grow
  Stress-pattern that runs 50 iterations of fill→spawn-waiter→
  yield_now→update_max→expect-wake. The yield_now hands off the
  scheduler immediately so the update races against the waiter's
  predicate-check window rather than after it has comfortably
  parked, maximizing the chance of hitting the bug if the lock
  acquisition is removed.
- Existing streaming_byte_limiter_update_max_bytes_in_flight_wakes_waiters
  continues to pass — covers the comfortably-parked-then-update path.

Validation: rch test pass — 5/5 StreamingByteLimiter tests, 37s, exit=0.

Closes coding_agent_session_search-wxsy8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
